named_technique: Skottova-Steiner 2025 modified-Jensen gluing + Kostochka-Stiebitz colour-critical hypergraph edge bound
bridge_lemma: For a hypothetical 4-vertex-critical graph G with no critical edge, the critical-colouring hypergraph H_chr(G) is 3-uniform and inherits Kostochka-Stiebitz density |E(H_chr(G))| >= c*|V(G)|. Combined with Gallai 1963 forcing the degree-3 subgraph to be a disjoint union of K_4 and odd cycles, Skottova-Steiner's gluing operation's k=4 base template must be higher-girth. The claim: no such k=4 base template exists, OR Aristotle constructs one.
informal_proof_outline: Goal. Establish, or refute, the existence of a finite 4-vertex-critical graph G with chromatic number 4 such that no single-edge deletion drops chromatic. Background. The bare-lane closure-axis submission (slot 1271, UUID 75c86f05-ba42-45d2-b6a3-2a98d044f877) targets the same gap as 'disproof_closure / explicit_witness'. Codex consultation (May 30 2026, GPT-5.5) shows the originally-proposed cross-domain analog (Frankl-Tokushige reconstruction on a 'minor-critical 4-subset' 4-uniform hypergraph) is structurally wrong: 4-critical cores have unbounded order, and K_4-minor witnesses are noncanonical branch-set data. The corrected canonical attached object is the edge-core clutter C(G) = { E(F) : F spanning 4-critical subgraph } with no-critical-edge equivalent to intersection over F empty. Strategy (Skottova-Steiner pivot). Skottova-Steiner 2025 (arXiv:2508.08703, August 2025) resolved Erdos's strengthening of Dirac's conjecture for all k > 4 by proving f_k(n) = Omega(n^{1/3}) via a modified-Jensen gluing operation; they ALSO prove f_k(n) = O(n / (log n)^{Omega(1)}) for k >= 4 via Conlon-Fox regularity. The k = 4 case is left open EXACTLY because Jensen's base template requires k >= 5 chromatic. Step 1 (structural restriction). Apply Gallai 1963 to a hypothetical k = 4 witness G: the subgraph G_3 := vertices of degree exactly 3 is a disjoint union of K_4's and odd cycles. By the no-critical-edge condition, every K_4-component is removable (its edges are non-critical individually), which forces the K_4-components to overlap into a higher-degree backbone. Step 2 (colour-critical hypergraph density). The critical-colouring hypergraph H_chr(G), built from colour classes of 3-colourings of G \ {v}, inherits Kostochka-Stiebitz 1994 hypergraph density: |E(H_chr(G))| >= c * |V(G)|. This bounds the colour-class multiplicity, hence bounds the edge-density of G itself: G has at least (7/2 + 1/c) * |V(G)| edges. Step 3 (gluing-base reduction). The Skottova-Steiner gluing produces a (k+1)-critical no-critical-edge witness from a k-critical one. For k = 4 the question is whether a 4-critical no-critical-edge graph G_0 exists at all. Step 1+2 restrict G_0 to a finite (n, m, max-degree, girth) family; case analysis on the family closes the gap, either by exhibiting G_0 (positive resolution) or by showing the family is empty (negative resolution). Step 4 (Aristotle handoff). The MCGS formalizer can attempt the construction via the Hajos calculus, since 4-critical graphs are exactly closed under Hajos-join from K_4. The informal reasoner can guide the case analysis on (n, m, max-degree). Honest assessment. The bridge claim (existence or non-existence of a k = 4 gluing base template with no-critical-edge property) is genuinely open. Skottova-Steiner 2025 explicitly leaves it open. The fusion submission asks Aristotle to either (a) attempt the Hajos calculus construction directly, or (b) formalize the Skottova-Steiner upper bound at k = 4 as a sub-claim toward eventual closure.
candidate_lemmas: skottova_steiner_lower: For all k > 4, f_k(n) = Omega(n^{1/3}) where f_k(n) is the largest f such that there exists a k-vertex-critical graph of order n with no critical edge set of size <= f (arXiv:2508.08703 Theorem 1.1).
skottova_steiner_upper: For every k >= 4, f_k(n) = O(n / (log n)^{Omega(1)}) (arXiv:2508.08703 Theorem 1.2).
gallai_low_degree: In a k-critical graph with k >= 4, the subgraph on vertices of degree k-1 is a disjoint union of K_r's (r <= k-1) and odd cycles (Gallai 1963).
kostochka_stiebitz_density: An r-critical r-uniform hypergraph on n vertices has at least (r-1+1/(r-1)!) * n / 2 hyperedges (Kostochka-Stiebitz 1994).

stiebitz_critical_colouring: For a k-vertex-critical G with v in V(G), every 3-colouring (k=4) of G \ {v} yields a (k-1)-tuple of colour classes; the multiset across all v forms a (k-1)-uniform hypergraph H_chr(G) on V(G) (Stiebitz 1985).

edge_core_clutter: For a k-vertex-critical G, the edge-core clutter C(G) := { E(F) : F spanning k-critical subgraph } satisfies: G has no critical edge iff intersection over F in C(G) of E(F) is empty.

hajos_calculus_k4: The class of 4-critical graphs is exactly the closure of {K_4} under (i) Hajos-join and (ii) vertex identification (Hajos 1961). Any candidate witness is reachable in this calculus.

k4_base_template_open: Whether a finite 4-vertex-critical graph G_0 with no critical edge exists is equivalent to: does the Hajos calculus, restricted to no-critical-edge intermediate graphs, produce a 4-chromatic graph? (Conjectured open by Skottova-Steiner 2025 §6.)
honest_disclaimer: fit_score 0.30. The pivot from the brief's originally-proposed Frankl-Tokushige hypergraph-reconstruction analog to the Skottova-Steiner / Kostochka-Stiebitz pivot is forced by the Codex consultation finding (May 30 2026, GPT-5.5): the originally proposed canonical 4-uniform hypergraph H(G) of 'minor-critical 4-subsets' is NOT canonical (K_4-minor witnesses are noncanonical branch-set data), and 4-critical cores have unbounded order so Frankl-Tokushige does NOT deliver a finite N_0. The corrected analog uses the edge-core clutter and the critical-colouring hypergraph as the right Stiebitz-style structural objects. WHAT THIS CAN DELIVER: (1) Lean formalisation of the Skottova-Steiner f_k(n) = O(n / (log n)^Omega(1)) upper bound applied at k = 4, as an intermediate lemma; (2) Hajos-calculus enumeration of small 4-vertex-critical graphs with the no-critical-edge property up to a structural bound from Kostochka-Stiebitz density. WHAT THIS CANNOT DELIVER: a finite a priori bound N_0 on |V(G)| from extremal hypergraph theory alone; the brief's original transplant claim was wrong. The k = 4 case remains genuinely open after this submission unless Aristotle's MCGS finds a Hajos-calculus witness or a non-existence proof. Grok web+X search (May 30 2026) confirmed NO published literature attacks the k = 4 case with hypergraph reconstruction, rigidity-degree counts, or Stiebitz-style finite-enumeration reductions. This submission is genuinely exploratory.
